mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of instances where the unemployment rate crosses above the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in new home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing demand cycle, which transmits to broader equity markets through financial sector stress and reduced consumer spending, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 during periods of supply chain bottlenecks or when inflation expectations exceed 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP growth reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which mechanically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters yield negative equity returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a ten-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 210.0, accelerating inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces monetary tightening, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of months where the consumer price index exceeds the 210.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered months during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in housing starts signal a deteriorating construction sector and reduced household wealth effects, which suppresses consumer confidence and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative equity returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 during periods of low mortgage rates.
